title: Rack of Lamb with a Senqu River Pistachio Crumb Crust
excerpt: I know that lamb is more expensive than other meat, but a treat every now and then can't do anyone any harm. I don't have the little bones cleaned, because we love the fat on the chops.

seo.title: Rack of Lamb with a Senqu River Pistachio Crumb Crust - Marinda Kook

html: I know that lamb is more expensive than other meat, but a treat every now and then can't do anyone any harm.  I don't have the little bones cleaned, because we love the fat on the chops.

recipe.title: Rack of Lamb with a Senqu River Pistachio Crumb Crust
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

items: 250 ml finely chopped pistachio nuts (I used the fantastic Senqu River nuts)

steps: Sear the fat side of the rack of lamb in a hot pan on the stove until the fat is nicely browned. Let the meat cool.
Spread the fat side of the chops with mustard.
Mix the rest of the ingredients and spread it over the mustard-covered section.
Place a rack inside a roasting dish and place the rack of lamb on the rack.
Bake uncovered at 180C for 15-20 min. The lamb will then still be nicely pink. If you'd like it more well done, leave it in the oven longer.
